title: Dilating a Point from the Origin

steps.instruction: A dilation resizes a figure by a scale factor from a center point. Scale factor > 1 enlarges; between 0 and 1 shrinks.
steps.work: Center of dilation: origin (0, 0). Scale factor: k.
steps.explanation: Each coordinate is multiplied by the scale factor.

steps.instruction: Dilate point (2, 5) by scale factor 3 from the origin.
steps.explanation: Both coordinates are multiplied by 3. The point moves farther from the origin.

steps.instruction: Dilate point (8, 4) by scale factor 1/2 from the origin.
steps.explanation: The point moves closer to the origin. The figure shrinks.

title: How Dilation Affects Lengths and Areas

steps.instruction: When you dilate by scale factor k, all lengths are multiplied by k.
steps.work: Original side = 5. Scale factor = 3. New side = 5 × 3 = 15.
steps.explanation: Every linear measurement changes by the scale factor.

steps.instruction: Area changes by k² (the square of the scale factor).
steps.work: Original area = 12 sq cm. Scale factor = 3. New area = 12 × 3² = 12 × 9 = 108 sq cm.
steps.explanation: Area is two-dimensional, so the scale factor applies twice.

steps.instruction: Dilation preserves shape (angles stay the same) but changes size.
steps.work: Angles: unchanged. Lengths: × k. Area: × k².
steps.explanation: Dilated figures are similar to the original.
